title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
description: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of San Francisco.

title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
description: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystem than the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App […]

title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event
description: Surprise! StrictlyVC San Francisco, which will kick off this year’s events lineup for TechCrunch on April 30 at the Sentro Filipino Cultural Center, is getting a new addition to its increasingly stacked lineup of speakers. Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga will join the rest of the lineup to discuss operating at scale in the age of AI.

title: Tim Cook is stepping down. What happens to Apple now?
description: Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystemthan the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App Store’s 30% cut is under pressure, the behind-the-scenes power Apple once […]
